title: Log Source Coverage Gaps — Expected Sourcetypes with Zero Events

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI DSS 12.10.1 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that PCI DSS 12.10.1 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.11.72: Log Source Coverage Gaps — Expected Sourcetypes with Zero Events.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.11.72' running on | metadata type=sourcetypes index=pci OR index=cde; lookup pci_required_sourcetypes.csv, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

dataSources: `| metadata type=sourcetypes index=pci OR index=cde`; lookup `pci_required_sourcetypes.csv`
spl:
| inputlookup pci_required_sourcetypes.csv
| fields required_sourcetype, required_index
| join type=left required_sourcetype [
    | metadata type=sourcetypes index=pci index=cde
    | rename sourcetype as required_sourcetype
    | eval seen_recent=if(recentTime>relative_time(now(),"-2h"),1,0)
    | fields required_sourcetype, seen_recent, totalCount
  ]
| where isnull(seen_recent) OR seen_recent=0
| table required_index, required_sourcetype, seen_recent, totalCount
description: Compares a reference list of required sourcetypes for PCI monitoring to last-hour ingestion so missing log categories are detected before an audit sample fails completeness tests.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for PCI DSS compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes cde, pci. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Curate lookup with owner contact per sourcetype. (2) Run hourly; exclude maintenance windows via secondary lookup. (3) Auto-ticket to platform ops. (4) Attach zero-gap monthly PDF for PCI evidence. Indexes required: `index=cde`, `index=pci`.
visualization: Table (missing sources), single value (missing count), heatmap (by required_index).
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
grandmaExplanation: We watch for compares a reference list of required sourcetypes for pci monitoring to last-hour ingestion so missing log categories are detected before an audit sample fails completeness tests. This matters because it catches problems that could lead to audit findings or compliance gaps.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), metadata searches.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `| metadata type=sourcetypes index=pci OR index=cde`; lookup `pci_required_sourcetypes.csv`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Curate lookup with owner contact per sourcetype. (2) Run hourly; exclude maintenance windows via secondary lookup. (3) Auto-ticket to platform ops. (4) Attach zero-gap monthly PDF for PCI evidence.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| inputlookup pci_required_sourcetypes.csv
| fields required_sourcetype, required_index
| join type=left required_sourcetype [
    | metadata type=sourcetypes index=pci index=cde
    | rename sourcetype as required_sourcetype
    | eval seen_recent=if(recentTime>relative_time(now(),"-2h"),1,0)
    | fields required_sourcetype, seen_recent, totalCount
  ]
| where isnull(seen_recent) OR seen_recent=0
| table required_index, required_sourcetype, seen_recent, totalCount
```

Understanding this SPL

**Log Source Coverage Gaps — Expected Sourcetypes with Zero Events (PCI DSS Req 10.2.1, 12.10.1)** — Compares a reference list of required sourcetypes for PCI monitoring to last-hour ingestion so missing log categories are detected before an audit sample fails completeness tests.

Documented **Data sources**: `| metadata type=sourcetypes index=pci OR index=cde`; lookup `pci_required_sourcetypes.csv`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263), metadata searches. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Loads rows via `inputlookup` (KV store or CSV lookup) for enrichment or reporting.
• Keeps or drops fields with `fields` to shape columns and size.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• Filters the current rows with `where isnull(seen_recent) OR seen_recent=0` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: required_index, required_sourcetype, seen_recent, totalCount.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (missing sources), single value (missing count), heatmap (by required_index).
